final-school-program-participation-repor.label: Final Capstone: Student Program Participation Audit
final-school-program-participation-repor.summary: Build a one-row-per-student audit that preserves students with no enrollments and measures the breadth of each student's participation.

sketch0.title: Capstone Brief: Audit Participation by Student

project.title: Final Capstone: Student Program Participation Audit

step2_add_counts.title: Step 2: Add Enrollment and Course Counts

step1_establish_grain.title: Step 1: Preserve the All-Student Grain
step1_establish_grain.prompt: Start from `students`, follow the complete relationship path with LEFT JOIN, and group so every student—including Liam—appears exactly once.
step1_establish_grain.hint: Run the previous working query, preserve its grain, and add only the metric or label requested in this step.

help.concept: Each capstone step preserves one row per student while extending the same four-table relationship path.
help.hint_1: The left side must remain `students` so the unenrolled student is never lost.
help.hint_2: Use distinct entity identifiers for course and department breadth, then verify Liam's zero-count row.

step1_establish_grain.starterCode: -- Build one row per student and preserve students with no enrollments
step1_establish_grain.solutionCode:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

step2_add_counts.title: Step 2: Add Enrollment and Course Counts
step2_add_counts.prompt: Add `enrollment_count` and distinct `course_count` while preserving one row per student.
step2_add_counts.hint: Run the previous working query, preserve its grain, and add only the metric or label requested in this step.

help.concept: Each capstone step preserves one row per student while extending the same four-table relationship path.
help.hint_1: The left side must remain `students` so the unenrolled student is never lost.
help.hint_2: Use distinct entity identifiers for course and department breadth, then verify Liam's zero-count row.

step2_add_counts.starterCode:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

-- Step 2: Add Enrollment and Course Counts
-- Add `enrollment_count` and distinct `course_count` while preserving one row per student.
step2_add_counts.solutionCode:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

main_py.content:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

-- Step 2: Add Enrollment and Course Counts
-- Add `enrollment_count` and distinct `course_count` while preserving one row per student.

SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

step3_add_distinct_students.title: Step 3: Measure Department Breadth
step3_add_distinct_students.prompt: Add distinct `department_count` to show how many departments each student participates in.
step3_add_distinct_students.hint: Run the previous working query, preserve its grain, and add only the metric or label requested in this step.

help.concept: Each capstone step preserves one row per student while extending the same four-table relationship path.
help.hint_1: The left side must remain `students` so the unenrolled student is never lost.
help.hint_2: Use distinct entity identifiers for course and department breadth, then verify Liam's zero-count row.

step3_add_distinct_students.starterCode:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

-- Step 3: Measure Department Breadth
-- Add distinct `department_count` to show how many departments each student participates in.
step3_add_distinct_students.solutionCode:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) AS department_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

main_py.content:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

-- Step 3: Measure Department Breadth
-- Add distinct `department_count` to show how many departments each student participates in.

SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) AS department_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

step4_label_rank.title: Step 4: Label and Rank Participation
step4_label_rank.prompt: Add `participation_status`: `Not enrolled` for zero enrollments, `Cross-disciplinary` for two or more departments, otherwise `Focused`. Sort by department breadth, enrollment count, and student name.
step4_label_rank.hint: Run the previous working query, preserve its grain, and add only the metric or label requested in this step.

help.concept: Each capstone step preserves one row per student while extending the same four-table relationship path.
help.hint_1: The left side must remain `students` so the unenrolled student is never lost.
help.hint_2: Use distinct entity identifiers for course and department breadth, then verify Liam's zero-count row.

step4_label_rank.starterCode:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) AS department_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

-- Step 4: Label and Rank Participation
-- Add `participation_status`: `Not enrolled` for zero enrollments, `Cross-disciplinary` for two or more departments, otherwise `Focused`. Sort by department breadth, enrollment count, and student name.
step4_label_rank.solutionCode:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) AS department_count,
       CASE
         WHEN COUNT(e.id) = 0 THEN 'Not enrolled'
         WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) >= 2 THEN 'Cross-disciplinary'
         ELSE 'Focused'
       END AS participation_status
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level
ORDER BY department_count DESC,
         enrollment_count DESC,
         student_name ASC;

main_py.content:
SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) AS department_count
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level;

-- Step 4: Label and Rank Participation
-- Add `participation_status`: `Not enrolled` for zero enrollments, `Cross-disciplinary` for two or more departments, otherwise `Focused`. Sort by department breadth, enrollment count, and student name.

SELECT s.name AS student_name,
       s.grade_level,
       COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) AS department_count,
       CASE
         WHEN COUNT(e.id) = 0 THEN 'Not enrolled'
         WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) >= 2 THEN 'Cross-disciplinary'
         ELSE 'Focused'
       END AS participation_status
FROM students AS s
LEFT JOIN enrollments AS e
  ON s.id = e.student_id
LEFT JOIN courses AS c
  ON e.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN departments AS d
  ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, s.grade_level
ORDER BY department_count DESC,
         enrollment_count DESC,
         student_name ASC;

sketch-intro.title: Build a new report grain for the final challenge
sketch-intro.bodyMarkdown:
The Module 2 project summarized one row per department. This capstone changes the question: the counseling team needs **one row per student**, including students who are not enrolled anywhere.

Your final report must contain:

- `student_name`
- `grade_level`
- `enrollment_count`
- `course_count`
- `department_count`
- `participation_status`

Follow the full path `students → enrollments → courses → departments`, preserve every student with `LEFT JOIN`, count distinct courses and departments, and verify that Liam appears with zero participation. This is a portfolio report because it combines relationship planning, unmatched-row preservation, result grain, distinct counting, labeling, and deterministic ranking in one query.
